title: Centaurea × saratoi
taxonomy: Centaurea × saratoi is a hybrid species within the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This plant is a cross between the Centaurea montana and Centaurea nigra species. As a hybrid, Centaurea × saratoi does not have a precise classification in the traditional sense, but it is often treated as a distinct species within the genus Centaurea. However, its classification can vary depending on the botanical source or classification system used. In some cases, Centaurea × saratoi may be considered a subspecies or a variety of one of its parent species.

benefits: Centaurea × saratoi, also known as Sarato's knapweed, has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of menopausal hot flashes. intro: Centaurea × saratoi, also known as Saracco's knapweed, is a hybrid perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia, as well as support digestive health. The bioactive constituents of Centaurea × saratoi include flavonoids, such as rutin and quercetin, as well as sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory activities. In herbal medicine, Centaurea × saratoi is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is often used to support the immune system and alleviate symptoms of stress and anxiety.
